intuition: We need to buy low and sell high, but the buy must come before the sell. Instead of checking all pairs in O(n2), we can track the minimum price seen so far as we scan left to right. At each point, the best profit if we sell today is today's price minus the minimum price seen so far.
approach: Single pass: maintain minPrice and maxProfit. For each price, update minPrice if it is lower, then update maxProfit with currentPrice minus minPrice.
hints: You must buy before you sell. Scanning left to right guarantees this.
At each day, what is the best profit if you sell today?
Track the minimum price seen so far as you iterate.
commonMistakes: Taking min of whole array and max of whole array — this ignores the buy-before-sell constraint.
Returning negative profit — if prices only fall, return 0.
author: arnav-goel
